sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Orange at [13,34], moved from [13,33]. The dark gray endpoint at [9,49] is still there. I need to keep moving the orange square toward it.
Let me check what white diamonds are still available to click. The path seems to be running out. Let me look at the remaining white diamonds and find the next one to click.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Orange at [13,34]. Need to find next white diamond.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
w_objs = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
print("White diamonds:")
for w in sorted(w_objs, key=lambda n: (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))):
    wc = sum(p[0] for p in w['boundary']) / len(w['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in w['boundary']) / len(w['boundary'])
    print(f"  ({wc[0]:.0f},{wc[1]:.0f}) pixels={w['pixels']}")

# Check orange nodes for endpoint
print("\nOrange nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] <= 5:
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']} center≈({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

    print(f"Orange now at ({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f})")
</parameter>
